title: Short-lived feature branches deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Short-lived feature branches; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Short-lived feature branches: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Creating many branches automatically prevents conflicts and guarantees changes are based on the latest target.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Branches provide named isolation, but developers must choose the base, keep scope coherent, update deliberately, test, and integrate through the agreed process.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Branches provide named isolation, but developers must choose the base, keep scope coherent, update deliberately, test, and integrate through the agreed process.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Short-lived feature branches instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Branches provide named isolation, but developers must choose the base, keep scope coherent, update deliberately, test, and integrate through the agreed process.
Creating many branches automatically prevents conflicts and guarantees changes are based on the latest target.

answer.explanation: Branches provide named isolation, but developers must choose the base, keep scope coherent, update deliberately, test, and integrate through the agreed process.
